title: Tabernaemontana gummifera
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana gummifera is classified under the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant belongs to the order Gentianales, which includes other flowering plant families such as the gentian and loganiaceae families. The genus Tabernaemontana itself is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical Asia and are known for their ornamental and medicinal properties. The species T. gummifera is native to Africa and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. It is also known as the 'Yellow Milkwood' due to its bright yellow latex and the wood of the tree.
uses: Tabernaemontana gummifera has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, primarily to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In some parts of Africa, Tabernaemontana gummifera is used to treat conditions such as malaria, typhoid, and ulcers. The plant's roots, leaves, and bark are often used in traditional medicine, and its extracts are also being studied for their potential antiviral properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Tabernaemontana gummifera.

These compounds are responsible for its medicinal properties, including antimalarial and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the plant has been found to contain other alkaloids like tabernaemontanine and 19-oxo-voacangine. side_effects: Tabernaemontana gummifera is a plant native to tropical Africa, and its side effects are largely unknown due to limited research. intro: Tabernaemontana gummifera, also known as the Indian periwinkle, is a plant species native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The health benefits of Tabernaemontana gummifera include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids like vobasine, tabernanthine, and vobtusine, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional herbal medicine, Tabernaemontana gummifera is commonly used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and fever, and to relieve pain and inflammation.
